title: record-level DBMS
text: record-level DBMS A database system that does not support the concept of fields (with the possible exception of fields within indexes). Data is stored and retrieved as records using their position in the file, and by indexes. The majority of record-level databases do not support a DDL or a DML. Many record-level DBMSs do not support a DDL or a DML. The Indexed Sequential Access Method (ISAM) allows records to be retrieved sequentially, ordered by their primary keys. The term ISAM-like DBMS is sometimes used to describe a record-level DBMS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
